scope: Current FastAPI router registration, route-local readiness surfaces, and lifecycle-hook binding gaps for the combined MaaS router plus direct legacy/v1 routers.

interpretation.route_import_only: The router is registered by src/core/app.py directly or through src.api.maas.endpoints.combined, but no app-bound startup/shutdown hook is proven for that router.
interpretation.production_lifespan_hook_defined_not_app: The router module has explicit startup/shutdown hooks referenced by src/core/production_lifespan.py, but current src/core/app.py does not bind production_lifespan as the FastAPI lifespan.
interpretation.always: Registered by current src/core/app.py regardless of MAAS_LIGHT_MODE; MAAS_LIGHT_MODE now changes version labelling, not router inclusion.
interpretation.none_explicit: The current router has no dedicated readiness endpoint; readiness must be inferred from narrower endpoint tests or higher-level gates, not from route registration alone.

light_mode.lifespan: Same FastAPI app construction; version label gains -light. Router inclusion is not gated by MAAS_LIGHT_MODE in current src/core/app.py.

non_light_mode.lifespan: Same FastAPI app construction with base version label. Current src/core/app.py does not pass lifespan=production_lifespan.

production_lifespan_runtime.binding_gap: Current src/core/app.py does not bind production_lifespan as FastAPI lifespan; production_lifespan.py defines startup/shutdown orchestration, but hook-defined modules cannot be treated as started by app import alone.

routers.label: nodes
routers.hidden_dependency: Combined-router node lifecycle routes depend on DB-backed MeshNode/MeshInstance state, node runtime credentials, approval/revocation state, heartbeat credentials, node-config access control, and operator heal wiring. The real Go-agent verifier proves this only against a temporary MaaS API and does not prove live customer traffic, production dataplane delivery, external reachability, production SLOs, or production readiness.

routers.label: auth
routers.hidden_dependency: Auth routes are registered through the combined router but this module has no explicit readiness endpoint. Runtime confidence comes from auth endpoint tests and downstream readiness gates rather than a route-local readiness handler.

routers.label: mesh
routers.hidden_dependency: Mesh lifecycle routes are registered through the combined router and expose claim gates on deploy/status/metrics/control surfaces, but there is no dedicated route-local readiness endpoint for the mesh module itself. This is a no explicit readiness endpoint boundary.

routers.label: billing
routers.hidden_dependency: Modular MaaS billing routes expose billing plans, usage, invoices, checkout, and claim gates, but the modular endpoint has no explicit readiness route; commercial readiness remains covered by the separate /api/v1/billing readiness surface.

routers.label: batman
routers.hidden_dependency: BATMAN observed-state/control routes are registered through the combined router and carry local-only claim gates, but the module has no explicit readiness endpoint distinct from its health/status responses.

routers.label: pilot
routers.hidden_dependency: Pilot signup/status/activation routes are registered through the combined router and emit bounded local API/DB evidence, but the module has no explicit readiness endpoint.

routers.label: playbooks
routers.hidden_dependency: Playbook readiness depends on memory delivery queues, token signing, durable playbook/ack database state, and audit-log persistence. It does not prove a node executed a playbook action.

routers.label: provisioning
routers.hidden_dependency: Provisioning readiness checks local DB write ability, EventBus availability, and auth dependency. It does not prove generated install commands ran, a node joined, dataplane traffic passed, or PQC/ZKP was negotiated.

routers.label: marketplace
routers.hidden_dependency: Marketplace readiness distinguishes route availability from state-changing database readiness. It does not prove live chain settlement, payment finality, or production revenue recognition.

routers.label: governance
routers.hidden_dependency: Governance readiness checks DB session shape, EventBus importability, SafeActuator importability, service identity, and optional production policy engine availability. It does not prove a proposal executed in production.

routers.label: analytics
routers.hidden_dependency: Analytics readiness checks DB aggregation and service construction, with Redis telemetry marked as a degraded dependency instead of production proof.

routers.label: agent-mesh
routers.hidden_dependency: Agent-mesh health-bot readiness depends on local health-bot surface/config, auth dependency, status payload shape, and non-dry-run execution token guard. It does not prove SOCKS reachability or executed self-heal commands.

routers.label: supply-chain
routers.hidden_dependency: Supply-chain readiness checks durable attestation/SBOM storage, audit-log persistence, compatibility SBOM registry, and eBPF adapter importability. It does not prove a kernel filter is loaded.

routers.label: policies
routers.hidden_dependency: Policy readiness checks DB-backed ACL policy surfaces and RBAC dependencies. It does not prove dataplane policy enforcement on nodes.

routers.label: telemetry
routers.hidden_dependency: Telemetry readiness checks DB session shape, MeshNode model, Redis/fallback cache, uptime tracker, reputation system, metrics export, and auth dependency. Cached topology or heartbeat observations remain local-only and not dataplane proof.

routers.label: compat
routers.hidden_dependency: Compat readiness covers alias wiring and delegated local dependencies for auth, mesh deploy, and billing aliases without executing delegated operations.

routers.label: vpn
routers.hidden_dependency: VPN readiness checks DB/session/model/config-generator/XUI/cache/auth/ZKP/production-env surfaces. It does not prove customer VPN traffic availability.

routers.label: users
routers.hidden_dependency: Users readiness checks DB/session/user model, password hashing, token generation, rate limiter, and admin-token configuration.

routers.label: swarm
routers.hidden_dependency: Swarm API readiness checks local registry, admin token, rate limiter, orchestrator import, task model, and vision engine import. It does not prove a live distributed swarm is healthy.

routers.label: ledger
routers.hidden_dependency: Ledger readiness checks local RAG surfaces, continuity file handle, verification evidence indexing methods, and EventBus trace indexing dependencies. It does not prove indexed data freshness or production readiness.

routers.label: swarm-orchestration
routers.hidden_dependency: Swarm orchestration readiness checks component imports, runtime state, task scheduler, and agent availability without proving production orchestration outcomes.

routers.label: vision-analytics
routers.hidden_dependency: Vision readiness checks local image/processor/topology/correction/upload surfaces only and does not prove production vision accuracy.

routers.label: service-identity-status
routers.hidden_dependency: Service identity readiness checks registry and redacted EventBus trace surfaces. It does not prove live SPIFFE, DID, wallet, or chain trust finality.

routers.label: billing-v1
routers.hidden_dependency: Commercial billing readiness checks Stripe checkout/webhook config, billing models, DB write methods, transport wrappers, VLESS link generation, and provisioning imports without proving live Stripe settlement.

routers.label: dashboard
routers.hidden_dependency: Dashboard readiness checks local dashboard/API helper surfaces and static payload dependencies without proving live node health.

routers.label: edge-computing
routers.hidden_dependency: Edge routes are directly registered, and edge_startup/edge_shutdown exist in production_lifespan.py. Current src/core/app.py does not bind production_lifespan as FastAPI lifespan, so app import alone does not prove startup hooks ran.

routers.label: event-sourcing
routers.hidden_dependency: Event-sourcing routes are directly registered, and event_sourcing_startup/event_sourcing_shutdown exist in production_lifespan.py. Current src/core/app.py does not bind production_lifespan as FastAPI lifespan, so app import alone does not prove startup hooks ran.

non_obvious_links.description: Most MaaS routers are now registered through src.api.maas.endpoints.combined.get_combined_router(), not individual _include_maas_router calls in src/core/app.py.
MAAS_LIGHT_MODE now affects the application version label but current src/core/app.py still includes the combined router and direct legacy/v1 routers.
production_lifespan.py still defines startup/shutdown orchestration, but src/core/app.py does not pass it into FastAPI, so startup_hook_completed fields are not proven by app import.
Several combined-router modules have bounded endpoint evidence but no dedicated readiness route. Their route registration must not be promoted to runtime readiness.
The real Go-agent smoke proves local temporary MaaS node-config/heartbeat/heal access paths, but it does not prove production lifecycle readiness or customer traffic delivery.
